meaning: adj.(指工作)艰苦的,费力的,(指人)勤劳的
examples: Keeping the garden tidy all year round can be a laborious task.
Photography was once an expensive, laborious ordeal reserved for life's greatest milestones.
Milton has just been lamenting the fact that the laborious days of the poet might not result in a sudden blaze of fame.
Animals, too, have been genetically engineered, mostly by laborious and imprecise trial-and-error breeding techniques.
The laborious process entails isolating a patient's T cells and inserting a new gene.
